* [Ian Goodfellow](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ian-goodfellow-b7187213)
  
  ### Ian Goodfellow
  
  1w
  
    + [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fian-goodfellow-b7187213_elorian-is-building-the-foundation-of-visual-activity-7448082380831191040-agwn&trk=public_profile__posts_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)
  
  [Ian Goodfellow shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_elorian-is-building-the-foundation-of-visual-activity-7448082380831191040-agwn)
  
  My former Brain colleague [Andrew Dai](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewdai?trk=public_profile__posts-text) just launched @ElorianAI
  
  [Andrew Dai](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewdai)
  
  ### Andrew Dai
  
  1w
  
  [Ian Goodfellow shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_elorian-is-building-the-foundation-of-visual-activity-7448082380831191040-agwn)
  
  After almost 14 years at Google with 12 of those in Brain and DeepMind, I’ve finally decided to hang up my Gemini swag and make the leap I dreamt of doing back when I was in Edinburgh. My co-founders and I have built [Elorian AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/elorian-ai?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , the first multimodal reasoning research and product lab founded and led by former pretraining, data and multimodal leads. To invest deeply in the research required to push this new frontier, we've raised $55 million from [Striker Venture Partners](https://www.linkedin.com/company/strikervp?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Menlo Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/menlo-ventures?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , and [Altimeter](https://www.linkedin.com/company/altimeter-capital?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , with participation from 49 palms and others and prominent AI researchers including [Jeff Dean](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeff-dean-8b212555?trk=public_profile__posts-text) .
  Over the last decade, it’s been an honor to be part of the growth of AI in this new generation of Bell Labs. Back then, [Ilya Sutskever](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilya-sutskever?trk=public_profile__posts-text) was still hand-writing C code to optimize GPU kernels while Jeff was helping me debug a threading issue during training by reading machine code. I wrote a paper with [Quoc V. Le](https://www.linkedin.com/in/quoc-v-le-319b5a8?trk=public_profile__posts-text) back then, which had a brief moment in the limelight when Sepp came up to me during my poster session and said ‘It works!’. Little did I know, that experiment is mostly what I would repeat (at larger and larger scale) for more than a decade.
  During all that, I believed text-based reasoning was all you need. After all, people who are blind can reason perfectly well. But after leading the data area for Gemini 3.0, I realized that even though frontier models are great at language and coding, they were still at the level of a child when given basic visual reasoning problems. Language alone gets you remarkably far, but the world is fundamentally visual, and so much understanding depends on being able to see and interpret what's in front of you. To me, this showed that we were coming at this from the wrong direction. In nature, pattern matching comes first, then visual reasoning, then language and finally advanced language (math & physics). Without this visual reasoning layer, we have models that can draw a pelican riding a bike with SVG code but can’t even count the number of fingers on a drawing of a hand.
  To solve this, my cofounders and I have built Elorian. The first multimodal reasoning research and product lab founded and led by former LLM pretraining, data and multimodal leads. We believe solving visual reasoning is the next biggest problem in AI and we aim to responsibly improve technology wherever better visual understanding can help. Ranging from engineering, through to robotics and agriculture. [https://lnkd.in/gS3wh9Gk](https://lnkd.in/gS3wh9Gk?trk=public_profile__posts-text) [x.com/ElorianAI](http://x.com/ElorianAI?trk=public_profile__posts-text) [https://lnkd.in/gf9uq3Ww](https://lnkd.in/gf9uq3Ww?trk=public_profile__posts-text) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_elorian-is-building-the-foundation-of-visual-activity-7448082380831191040-agwn) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_elorian-is-building-the-foundation-of-visual-activity-7448082380831191040-agwn)
  
  [Elorian is building the foundation of visual reasoning](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Eyoutube%2Ecom%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DYlvfNpOMeOY&urlhash=U-Mh)
  
  Elorian is building the foundation of visual reasoning

  [Yoshua Bengio](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/yoshuabengio)
  
  ### Yoshua Bengio
  
  6mo
  
  [Ian Goodfellow reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/yoshuabengio_international-ai-safety-report-activity-7384181383356682242-GnXu)
  
  In my role as Chair of the International AI Safety Report, an effort backed by over 30 countries and international organisations including the [European Union](https://be.linkedin.com/company/european-union?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [OECD - OCDE](https://fr.linkedin.com/company/organisation-eco-cooperation-development-organisation-cooperation-developpement-eco?trk=public_profile__posts-text) and [United Nations](https://www.linkedin.com/company/united-nations?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , I work with 100 researchers to help policymakers understand the capabilities and risks of general-purpose AI. The field is clearly changing far too quickly for a single annual report to suffice. That’s why today we’re introducing Key Updates: shorter, focused reports on critical developments in AI that will be published between editions of the full report. Our first Key Update focuses on advancements in AI capabilities, and what they mean for AI safety. You can read it here: [https://lnkd.in/eKVGF7dy](https://lnkd.in/eKVGF7dy?trk=public_profile__posts-text) Some of the key findings it covers include:
  ➡️ Impressive performance improvements. Several AI systems can now solve International Mathematical Olympiad problems at gold medal level and complete a majority of problems in several databases of real-world software engineering tasks.
  ➡️ The rise of “reasoning” models. Recent gains have come mainly from training and deployment techniques that allow AI models to generate interim steps before producing final answers. This demonstrates that AI capabilities can advance significantly through post-training techniques and additional computing power at inference time, not just through scaling model size.
  ➡️ Some signals of real-world adoption. In a recent StackOverflow survey, a majority of software developers report using AI tools daily to help design experiments, process data, and write reports. Yet we still don’t know much about AI use in many other domains, nor crucially about how AI use affects productivity overall.
  ➡️ Stronger safeguards from developers. Leading AI developers recently activated enhanced protections on their most capable models as a precautionary measure, given possibilities like misuse to build weapons.
  ➡️ Emerging oversight challenges. AI models increasingly demonstrate an ability to distinguish evaluation tasks from real-world tasks, possibly complicating our ability to reliably test their capabilities before deployment.
  These developments raise further questions about control, monitoring, and governance as AI systems become more capable. [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/yoshuabengio_international-ai-safety-report-activity-7384181383356682242-GnXu) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/yoshuabengio_international-ai-safety-report-activity-7384181383356682242-GnXu)
  
  [International AI Safety Report](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Finternationalaisafetyreport%2Eorg%2F&urlhash=ACXE)

This is a topic that has excited me for a long time—-if you look at my original NIPS 2014 talk on GANs, simulations were one of the reasons I wanted to develop better ability to generate samples [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_moonlake-ai-is-launching-today-focused-on-activity-7379209735843139584-cyr9)
  
  [Fan-Yun Sun](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sunfanyun)
  
  ### Fan-Yun Sun
  
  6mo
  
  [Ian Goodfellow shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_moonlake-ai-is-launching-today-focused-on-activity-7379209735843139584-cyr9)
  
  We've just raised a $28M seed round to put interactive world-building in everyone's hands.
  My cofounder [Sharon Lee](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sharonleeyen?trk=public_profile__posts-text) and I converged on the same observation a few months ago: models today are hitting a wall– they lack the practical simulations needed to learn and improve through reinforcement learning. Creators are trapped by complex tools that make building games feel impossibly hard.
  At [Moonlake AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/moonlake-ai?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , we're building reasoning models that generate simulations and games—so anyone can create interactive experiences, bring wild ideas to life, and share unforgettable moments with their friends.
  Moonlake's models and agents don’t just generate— they reason through which representations, tools, and approaches to use for each unique interactive experience.
  We are a team made up of Stanford CS PhDs, Math Olympiad, ACM ICPC medalists, and researchers with best paper award. We’re hiring for designers, engineers and researchers who are excited about building in the intersection of code generation, (3D) vision and graphics, and RL. – Big thanks to our earliest believers: [Mo Islam](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mo-islam-66082332?trk=public_profile__posts-text) from Threshold Ventures (formerly DFJ), [Shaun Johnson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shaunbjohnson?trk=public_profile__posts-text) and [Christopher Manning](https://www.linkedin.com/in/christopher-manning-011575?trk=public_profile__posts-text) from AIX Ventures, and [Elvin Hao](https://www.linkedin.com/in/elvinhao?trk=public_profile__posts-text) and [Artem Barsukov](https://www.linkedin.com/in/artem-barsukov-a4725337?trk=public_profile__posts-text) from NVentures (Nvidia’s venture capital arm), [Choon Chong Tay](https://sg.linkedin.com/in/choon-chong-tay-a60b571a?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Yazhuo Chloe Chen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/yazhuo-chloe-chen-441573262?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , alongside exception angles: [Ian Goodfellow](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ian-goodfellow-b7187213?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Researcher, DeepMind) [Steve Chen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/stevechen2?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Founder, Youtube)
  Naval Ravikant (Founder, Angelist) [Jeff Dean](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeff-dean-8b212555?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Chief Scientist, Google/Gemini) [Guillermo Rauch](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rauchg?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Founder, Vercel) [Christian Laforte](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/claforte?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (ex-CTO of Stability AI)
  Emery Wells (Founder, [Frame.io](http://Frame.io?trk=public_profile__posts-text) ) [Julien Chaumond](https://www.linkedin.com/in/julienchaumond?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Cofounder, HuggingFace) [Victor Mustar](https://fr.linkedin.com/in/victor-mustar-22466951?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Head of Product, HuggingFace) [Paul Daugherty](https://www.linkedin.com/in/paul-r-daugherty?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (ex-CEO Accenture) [William Freiberg](https://www.linkedin.com/in/william-freiberg-02034850?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (CEO of Crux) [Jared Leto](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jared-leto-468270208?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Angel Investor in OpenAI, SSI) [Mercedes Bent](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mercedesbent?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Venture Partner, LightSpeed) [Vanessa Larco](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vanessalarco?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Venture Partner, NEA) [Tony Wang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tonywang1?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Managing Partner, 500 Global) [Sudarshan Sridharan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/sudarshan-sridharan?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (General Partner, SF1) [Panos Madamopoulos-Moraris](https://www.linkedin.com/in/panosmm?trk=public_profile__posts-text) (Founder Exoscaleton)
  and many more.
  –
  Comment “Join Moonlake” if you want your application to be fast-tracked.
  Comment “Moonlaker” if you’d like to get early access to our product
  Check out our mission statement video in the comments 👇 [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_moonlake-ai-is-launching-today-focused-on-activity-7379209735843139584-cyr9) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_moonlake-ai-is-launching-today-focused-on-activity-7379209735843139584-cyr9)

  ### Ian Goodfellow
  
  8mo
  
    + [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fian-goodfellow-b7187213_snowglobe-from-guardrails-ai-brings-the-kind-activity-7361809577408950273-cQtF&trk=public_profile__posts_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)
  
  [Ian Goodfellow shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_snowglobe-from-guardrails-ai-brings-the-kind-activity-7361809577408950273-cQtF)
  
  Snowglobe from Guardrails AI brings the kind of simulation based testing that has worked for self-driving cars to chatbots
  
  [Shreya Rajpal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shreya-rajpal)
  
  ### Shreya Rajpal
  
  8mo
  
  [Ian Goodfellow shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_snowglobe-from-guardrails-ai-brings-the-kind-activity-7361809577408950273-cQtF)
  
  Today we’re announcing ❄️ Snowglobe - the simulation engine for AI chatbots!
  Snowglobe makes it easy to simulate realistic user conversations at scale so you can reveal the blind spots where your chatbots fail, and generate labeled datasets for finetuning them.
  We built Snowglobe to solve a problem that we ran into again and again through our journey building Guardrails for the last two years — evaluating AI agents is very challenging. If you spend days and weeks manually creating test scenarios for your chatbots, Snowglobe generates hundreds of realistic user conversations in minutes.
  How do you even formulate a test plan for evaluating something that can take infinite inputs? How do you deal with the many edge cases that break AI chatbots in prod all the time?
  Interestingly, self driving cars had the exact same problem. They built high fidelity simulation environments to systematically test cars under a wide range of scenarios. Waymo had 20+ million miles on real roads, but 20+ BILLION miles in sim so they had the confidence needed to ship.
  Today, we’re excited to bring that same tooling to AI agents with the general availability of Snowglobe! [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_snowglobe-from-guardrails-ai-brings-the-kind-activity-7361809577408950273-cQtF) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_snowglobe-from-guardrails-ai-brings-the-kind-activity-7361809577408950273-cQtF)

[...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_its-great-to-see-these-open-reproducible-activity-7295638653345640449-Pr9A)
  
  [Shreya Rajpal](https://www.linkedin.com/in/shreya-rajpal)
  
  ### Shreya Rajpal
  
  1y
  
  [Ian Goodfellow shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_its-great-to-see-these-open-reproducible-activity-7295638653345640449-Pr9A)
  
  We’ve come a long way since we first launched the [Guardrails AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/guardrailsai?trk=public_profile__posts-text) open source in 2023. Nowadays, guardrails are a pretty standard part of centralized AI platforms everywhere, and most organizations add a guardrailing service around their centralized AI gateways.
  BUT — how performant are these guardrails really?! I’m CONSTANTLY surprised by how little information there is about this..
  To remedy this, today I’m really excited to announce the Guardrails Index — a benchmark comparing the efficacy of popular guardrails (including ours) on 6 of the most popular AI safeguarding tasks, incl. Hallucinations, PII, Jailbreaking and more. For each task, we either created or curated a dataset, and did a fine grained analysis of how guardrails from different companies performed on the dataset.
  Take a look at the link below to learn more! [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_its-great-to-see-these-open-reproducible-activity-7295638653345640449-Pr9A) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/ian-goodfellow-b7187213_its-great-to-see-these-open-reproducible-activity-7295638653345640449-Pr9A)

  [Jonathan Citrin](https://uk.linkedin.com/in/jonathan-citrin-320240155)
  
  ### Jonathan Citrin
  
  1y
  
  [Ian Goodfellow reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jonathan-citrin-320240155_github-google-deepmindtorax-torax-tokamak-activity-7206622757415981056-n-OO)
  
  Excited to announce the release of TORAX, a new open-source tokamak core transport simulator! Building on ideas developed in the fusion community for tokamak scenario modeling, pulse planning, and control, TORAX employs the Python JAX framework to enable more efficient simulations and new powerful workflows.
  🚀 Fast: JAX's JIT compilation provides computational efficiency and seamlessly executes on multiple backends including CPU and GPU.
  📐 Differentiable: Automatic differentiation, even through complex nonlinear functions like ML-surrogates, allows for gradient-based nonlinear PDE solving, optimization, and sensitivity analysis.
  🤖 Seamless ML Integration: JAX's native support for neural network development and inference makes it straightforward to couple TORAX with machine learned surrogates of complex physics models.
  📦 Modularity: Python, with its ease-of-use and wide adoption, facilitates coupling within various workflows and to additional physics models.
  What can you do with TORAX?
  \- Predict scenarios and interpret physics with high throughput.
  \- Explore wide parameter spaces for scenario design and optimization.
  \- Accelerate development of model-based controllers for tokamaks.
  \- Facilitate uncertainty quantification and sensitivity analysis.
  This is just the beginning for TORAX. The roadmap envisions expanding the physics scope, incorporating a wider range of ML-surrogates and developing application workflows. We believe that through working together with the fusion energy community, we can make TORAX an invaluable tool for accelerating progress.
  TORAX is open-source and freely available on GitHub
  ➡ [https://lnkd.in/ebAsVXvX](https://lnkd.in/ebAsVXvX?trk=public_profile__posts-text) Documentation can be found on ReadTheDocs
  ➡ [https://lnkd.in/eGvj8B7t](https://lnkd.in/eGvj8B7t?trk=public_profile__posts-text) Paper hot off the press on arXiv
  ➡ [arxiv.org/abs/2406.06718](http://arxiv.org/abs/2406.06718?trk=public_profile__posts-text) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jonathan-citrin-320240155_github-google-deepmindtorax-torax-tokamak-activity-7206622757415981056-n-OO) [...more](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jonathan-citrin-320240155_github-google-deepmindtorax-torax-tokamak-activity-7206622757415981056-n-OO)

  ### Andrew Dai
  
  1w
  
  [Ian Goodfellow liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/andrewdai_elorian-is-building-the-foundation-of-visual-activity-7448069840642220032-O0eb)
  
  After almost 14 years at Google with 12 of those in Brain and DeepMind, I’ve finally decided to hang up my Gemini swag and make the leap I dreamt of doing back when I was in Edinburgh. My co-founders and I have built [Elorian AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/elorian-ai?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , the first multimodal reasoning research and product lab founded and led by former pretraining, data and multimodal leads. To invest deeply in the research required to push this new frontier, we've raised $55 million from [Striker Venture Partners](https://www.linkedin.com/company/strikervp?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [Menlo Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/menlo-ventures?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , and [Altimeter](https://www.linkedin.com/company/altimeter-capital?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , with participation from 49 palms and others and prominent AI researchers including [Jeff Dean](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeff-dean-8b212555?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) .
  Over the last decade, it’s been an honor to be part of the growth of AI in this new generation of Bell Labs. Back then, [Ilya Sutskever](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ilya-sutskever?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) was still hand-writing C code to optimize GPU kernels while Jeff was helping me debug a threading issue during training by reading machine code. I wrote a paper with [Quoc V. Le](https://www.linkedin.com/in/quoc-v-le-319b5a8?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) back then, which had a brief moment in the limelight when Sepp came up to me during my poster session and said ‘It works!’. Little did I know, that experiment is mostly what I would repeat (at larger and larger scale) for more than a decade.
  During all that, I believed text-based reasoning was all you need. After all, people who are blind can reason perfectly well. But after leading the data area for Gemini 3.0, I realized that even though frontier models are great at language and coding, they were still at the level of a child when given basic visual reasoning problems. Language alone gets you remarkably far, but the world is fundamentally visual, and so much understanding depends on being able to see and interpret what's in front of you. To me, this showed that we were coming at this from the wrong direction. In nature, pattern matching comes first, then visual reasoning, then language and finally advanced language (math & physics). Without this visual reasoning layer, we have models that can draw a pelican riding a bike with SVG code but can’t even count the number of fingers on a drawing of a hand.
  To solve this, my cofounders and I have built Elorian. The first multimodal reasoning research and product lab founded and led by former LLM pretraining, data and multimodal leads. We believe solving visual reasoning is the next biggest problem in AI and we aim to responsibly improve technology wherever better visual understanding can help. Ranging from engineering, through to robotics and agriculture. [https://lnkd.in/gS3wh9Gk](https://lnkd.in/gS3wh9Gk?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [x.com/ElorianAI](http://x.com/ElorianAI?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) [https://lnkd.in/gf9uq3Ww](https://lnkd.in/gf9uq3Ww?trk=public_profile__reactions-text)
  
  [Elorian is building the foundation of visual reasoning](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Eyoutube%2Ecom%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DYlvfNpOMeOY&urlhash=U-Mh)
  
  Elorian is building the foundation of visual reasoning

  I once made the shortlist for a tenure-track position at Stanford. After months of waiting, they chose not to hire anyone that year. Let me tell you... it was crushing.
  But when I was honest with myself, the main reason I'd wanted it was validation. It was proof that I'd "made it."
  Walking away from that mentality was one of the hardest (and best!) things I ever did. I joined Google Brain, later moved to Meta Reality Labs, and kept a foot in academia as an adjunct at Stanford. Turns out you CAN build a life in science that actually fits you — it just might not look like the one you were told to want.
  Neuron asked me to tell that story. If you're early in your career and wrestling with similar questions, I hope some of it helps.

  Today we’re sharing two major milestones for [Samaya AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samaya-ai?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) : the launch of the Agent Control Plane (ACP) and new investment from NVentures ( [NVIDIA](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nvidia?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) VC arm) and [Databricks](https://www.linkedin.com/company/databricks?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) Ventures!
  𝗔𝗜 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗪𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗲𝘁 𝘀𝗵𝗼𝘂𝗹𝗱𝗻'𝘁 𝗿𝗲𝗴𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘀 𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗲𝗮𝗻.
  True AI Agents in finance require sophisticated cause-and-effect reasoning — pinpointing how tariffs impact a specific portfolio, mapping how the AI boom ripples through global supply chains, and translating all of that into investment conviction. This reasoning is carefully grounded in each investor's unique context: their data, their risk frameworks, their individual thesis.
  The Agent Control Plane (ACP) is a new architecture for building personalized, long-horizon, AI Agents. These Agents can be "always on" and execute autonomously for hours, all while embedding institutional context so that every investor can act on what's unique to them.
  The investment from NVentures ( [NVIDIA](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nvidia?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) 's venture arm) and [Databricks](https://www.linkedin.com/company/databricks?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) will further support the development of the ACP, and is based on a shared belief that verticalization is the key unlock for building AI Agents that can deliver on high stakes financial services outcomes 🚀

  Excited to share that I joined Amazon AGI Labs a few months ago to work on RL for autonomous agents!
  Recently, I’ve been particularly interested in the computer use problem and how we can fundamentally simplify the way people interact with computers.
  On one of my family trips, I saw how much trouble my mom was having with seemingly simple phone and computer tasks. It became clear to me that as we advance these AI systems, we also have a responsibility in bringing along previous generations for the journey and I’m certain digital agents will play a crucial role.
  This space is still early but our lab strongly believes that large scale RL will be a key enabler in reliability and bridging the gap between current computer use models and human level performance. Last month, we released a significant upgrade to Nova Act by training a frontier level browser agent via RLVR across a large set of diverse environments and tasks ( [https://lnkd.in/gZ9sBaZa](https://lnkd.in/gZ9sBaZa?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) ) and we’re aiming to dramatically improve and expand our model’s capabilities in the coming year.
  If pushing the frontier of autonomous agents in the digital and physical worlds sounds exciting to you, join us! We’re a relatively small lab and are actively hiring across the board: [https://lnkd.in/gghNvMr6](https://lnkd.in/gghNvMr6?trk=public_profile__reactions-text)
  
  [We’re building useful general intelligence.](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=https%3A%2F%2Flabs%2Eamazon%2Escience%2Fcareers&urlhash=0Rzz)

## Publications

* ### [On the Challenges of Physical Implementations of RBMs](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Eaaai%2Eorg%2Focs%2Findex%2Ephp%2FAAAI%2FAAAI14%2Fpaper%2Fview%2F8608&urlhash=KLH1&trk=public_profile_publication-title "On the Challenges of Physical Implementations of RBMs")
  
  #### Proc. AAAI 2014, pp. 1199-1205 June 6, 2014
  
  Restricted Boltzmann machines (RBMs) are powerful machine learning models, but learning and some kinds of inference in the model require sampling-based approximations, which, in classical digital computers, are implemented using expensive MCMC. Physical computation offers the opportunity to reduce the costof sampling by building physical systems whose natural dynamics correspond to drawing samples from the desired RBM distribution. Such a system avoids the burn-in and mixing cost of a Markov… Show more
  
  Restricted Boltzmann machines (RBMs) are powerful machine learning models, but learning and some kinds of inference in the model require sampling-based approximations, which, in classical digital computers, are implemented using expensive MCMC. Physical computation offers the opportunity to reduce the costof sampling by building physical systems whose natural dynamics correspond to drawing samples from the desired RBM distribution. Such a system avoids the burn-in and mixing cost of a Markov chain. However, hardware implementations of this variety usually entail limitations such as low-precision and limited range of the parameters and restrictions on the size and topology of the RBM. We conduct software simulations to determine how harmful each of these restrictions is. Our simulations are based on the D-Wave Two computer, but the issues we investigate arise in most forms of physical computation.Our findings suggest that designers of new physical computing hardware and algorithms for physical computers should focus their efforts on overcoming the limitations imposed by the topology restrictions of currently existing physical computers. Show less
  
  [See publication](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Fwww%2Eaaai%2Eorg%2Focs%2Findex%2Ephp%2FAAAI%2FAAAI14%2Fpaper%2Fview%2F8608&urlhash=KLH1&trk=public_profile_publication-button "On the Challenges of Physical Implementations of RBMs")
* ### [Generative Adversarial Networks](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Farxiv%2Eorg%2Fabs%2F1406%2E2661&urlhash=dcmx&trk=public_profile_publication-title "Generative Adversarial Networks")
  
  #### NIPS 2014 Jun 2014
  
  We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than G. The training procedure for G is to maximize the probability of D making a mistake. This framework corresponds to a minimax two-player game. In the space of arbitrary functions G and D, a… Show more
  
  We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than G. The training procedure for G is to maximize the probability of D making a mistake. This framework corresponds to a minimax two-player game. In the space of arbitrary functions G and D, a unique solution exists, with G recovering the training data distribution and D equal to 1/2 everywhere. In the case where G and D are defined by multilayer perceptrons, the entire system can be trained with backpropagation. There is no need for any Markov chains or unrolled approximate inference networks during either training or generation of samples. Experiments demonstrate the potential of the framework through qualitative and quantitative evaluation of the generated samples. Show less
  
  Other authors

* ### [Multi-digit Number Recognition from Street View Imagery using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks](https://www.linkedin.com/redir/redirect?url=http%3A%2F%2Farxiv%2Eorg%2Fabs%2F1312%2E6082&urlhash=tQRn&trk=public_profile_publication-title "Multi-digit Number Recognition from Street View Imagery using Deep Convolutional Neural Networks")
  
  #### ICLR 2014 April 13, 2014
  
  Recognizing arbitrary multi-character text in unconstrained natural photographs is a hard problem. In this paper, we address an equally hard sub-problem in this domain viz. recognizing arbitrary multi-digit numbers from Street View imagery. Traditional approaches to solve this problem typically separate out the localization, segmentation, and recognition steps. In this paper we propose a unified approach that integrates these three steps via the use of a deep convolutional neural network that… Show more
  
  Recognizing arbitrary multi-character text in unconstrained natural photographs is a hard problem. In this paper, we address an equally hard sub-problem in this domain viz. recognizing arbitrary multi-digit numbers from Street View imagery. Traditional approaches to solve this problem typically separate out the localization, segmentation, and recognition steps. In this paper we propose a unified approach that integrates these three steps via the use of a deep convolutional neural network that operates directly on the image pixels. We employ the DistBelief implementation of deep neural networks in order to train large, distributed neural networks on high quality images. We find that the performance of this approach increases with the depth of the convolutional network, with the best performance occurring in the deepest architecture we trained, with eleven hidden layers. We evaluate this approach on the publicly available SVHN dataset and achieve over 96% accuracy in recognizing complete street numbers. We show that on a per-digit recognition task, we improve upon the state-of-the-art, achieving 97.84% accuracy. We also evaluate this approach on an even more challenging dataset generated from Street View imagery containing several tens of millions of street number annotations and achieve over 90% accuracy. To further explore the applicability of the proposed system to broader text recognition tasks, we apply it to synthetic distorted text from reCAPTCHA. reCAPTCHA is one of the most secure reverse turing tests that uses distorted text to distinguish humans from bots. We report a 99.8% accuracy on the hardest category of reCAPTCHA. Our evaluations on both tasks indicate that at specific operating thresholds, the performance of the proposed system is comparable to, and in some cases exceeds, that of human operators. Show less

## Projects

* ### [pylearn2](https://github.com/lisa-lab/pylearn2?trk=public_profile_project-title "pylearn2")
  
  #### Sep 2010 \- Present
  
  Pylearn2 is a machine learning library for machine learning researchers. It is designed to be user-friendly (to make coding easy) but expects a high level of machine learning expertise. Pylearn2 prioritizes flexibility, so that a researcher can run nearly any imagineable experiment. This is in contrast to for example scikits-learn, which prioritizes ease of use for non-experts and simplicity, and is designed to be a black box that works well rather than a platform for experimentation.
